differential_diagnoses: Hypervirulent Klebsiella pneumoniae infection with metastatic endophthalmitis and liver abscess
Disseminated fungal infection (e.g., Candida or Aspergillus)
Malignancy with paraneoplastic or metastatic sequelae
next_steps: Perform aspiration of the hepatic mass for culture, Gram stain, and pathology
Perform diagnostic vitreous tap (vitrectomy) for microbiology and PCR
Obtain follow-up blood, urine, and sputum cultures
Perform transesophageal echocardiography to rule out occult endocarditis
Initiate or optimize targeted antimicrobial therapy pending culture results
Consult Ophthalmology for management of endophthalmitis and potential intravitreal injections
Consider biopsy of suspicious pulmonary nodules if diagnosis remains elusive

differential_diagnoses: Hypervirulent Klebsiella pneumoniae (hvKP) syndrome with metastatic endophthalmitis and hepatic abscess
Disseminated fungal infection (e.g., Candida or Aspergillus)
next_steps: Obtain definitive microbiologic samples (blood cultures, hepatic abscess aspiration, vitrectomy for vitreous fluid analysis)
Perform diagnostic imaging (transesophageal echocardiography, CT/MRI of affected areas if not already done)
Evaluate for underlying predisposing conditions, particularly undiagnosed diabetes mellitus (HbA1c) and HIV status
Consult Ophthalmology immediately for potential vitrectomy and intravitreal antibiotic administration
Tailor broad-spectrum antibiotic therapy based on Gram stain and early culture results (prioritizing coverage for hvKP)
Manage supportive care for severe systemic sepsis and ocular preservation
